steps.title: Understand what x-intercepts mean for a cubic
steps.content: The question asks for the number of distinct values of $x$ where $f(x) = 0$, which are the x-intercepts. For a cubic function that has a local maximum and local minimum (as shown here), the graph can cross the x-axis either 1 or 3 times.

steps.title: Count the x-intercepts on the graph
steps.content:
Trace the curve from left to right and count how many times it crosses the x-axis:
  • One crossing occurs on the left side of the local maximum (curve transitions from below to above the axis)
  • One crossing occurs between the local maximum and local minimum (curve transitions from above to below the axis)
  • One crossing occurs on the right side of the local minimum (curve transitions from below to above the axis)
The graph displays exactly three x-intercepts. That matches choice C — C is correct.
